[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2014
[TITLE]: Humboldt Polar Bear Arrested During New York City Flood Wall Street Protests | Lost Coast Outpost | Humboldt County News
[METADATA/INTRO]: Save the #Arctic, save the #polarbear. #FloodWallStreet pic.twitter.com/FsaOBLqEJG — Center for Bio Div (@CenterForBioDiv) September 22, 2014...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: Our local polar bear’s rabble rouser status was further solidified Monday night. At a certain point, police had had enough and ordered the crowd to vamoose. They did not. In the end, around 100 protesters were arrested and our fuzzy friend submitted to having his paws cuffed behind his back (see below). 
 Police have arrested the polar bear #FloodWallStreet pic.twitter.com/jKq7byFUxG 
 — Jordan Mammo (@jordanmammo) September 22, 2014 
 Hear chants to “Save the Polar Bear!” in the clip below. 
 The Guardian reports the bear made bail and was released from human jail around 2 a.m. this morning (see below). 
 No word yet on how bearable his stay behind bars was.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2014-09-23
[TITLE]: From activism to arrest: one polar bear's adventure with Flood Wall Street | Climate crisis | The Guardian
[METADATA/INTRO]: The polar bear joined the Flood Wall Street protests on Monday in New York City. The bear danced with the crowds as they descended into the city’s financial district, the same area where Occupy Wall Street demonstrated (albeit without bears)....
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: The polar bear joined the Flood Wall Street protests on Monday in New York City. The bear danced with the crowds as they descended into the city’s financial district, the same area where Occupy Wall Street demonstrated (albeit without bears). 
 The bear joined a sit-in, during which protesters desperately tried to tell a police officer about the connection between capitalism and environmental destruction. Not only did the officer not sign their petition, he ruined the bear’s afternoon by not making eye contact at all. 
 The bear rested for a moment with two Captains Planet and linked arms with a man itching his goatee.

[PUBLISHED DATE]: 2018-11-25
[TITLE]: Was a Man in Bear Costume Arrested for Tearing Apart Tents of Black Friday Campers? | Snopes.com
[METADATA/INTRO]: In the last few decades, Black Friday (the derisive name bestowed upon the day after Thanksgiving in the U.S.) has morphed from a busy sales day marking the start of the holiday shopping season to a spectacle driven by retailers'...
[BEST EVIDENCE CHUNK]: The tent picture and its fictional backstory was eventually combined with an unrelated photograph from 2014 showing a man in a polar bear costume being arrested during Flood Wall Street, an environmental protest staged in downtown Manhattan: 
 For now, urban Black Friday shoppers remain safe from being terrorized by pranksters sporting ursine garb. No one is necessarily safe from digital manipulators armed with bear pictures, though.
